bottom_line.verdict: No local follow-up beats the current family mainline. The anchor5 far-z seed family keeps the unified mainline lead through `zseed_l5_neg6_plus_relaymax_unified_l9y2p5`, but it still misses the absolute max-frontier crown.

scientific_conclusion: Local ridge-follow-up confirms the anchor5 far-z seed family is stable rather than fragile. The best new local landing `zseed_l5_neg5p5_plus_relaymax_unified_l9y2p625` reaches 9.346 / 0.724 / 99.577. Against the current mainline `zseed_l5_neg6_plus_relaymax_unified_l9y2p5` = 9.343 / 0.693 / 99.576, this gives Δmean -0.004 and Δmax -0.000. Against the entry-conditioned frontier `entryrelay_l8x1_l9y1_unifiedcore` = 9.614 / 1.169 / 99.574, it still trails the entry-conditioned frontier by 0.002 on max. The local shape is also narrow rather than broad: the mild neg5.5 / l9y2.625 interpolation stays near the winner, whereas pushing to neg5.75 or neg6.25 causes a clear max collapse. The bottom-line read is therefore: No local follow-up beats the current family mainline. The anchor5 far-z seed family keeps the unified mainline lead through `zseed_l5_neg6_plus_relaymax_unified_l9y2p5`, but it still misses the absolute max-frontier crown.
